title: results_registry manifest row
description: Schema for one row of results_registry/manifest.tsv. Each row points at exactly one cached numerical artifact (a pairwise FST/dxy/theta table, a per-candidate Q matrix, a per-candidate F matrix, or a per-interval ancestry summary) and carries the provenance needed to reproduce it. Foreign keys into sample_registry (who) and interval_registry (where) are strings referencing the PKs of those tables; they must resolve at write time or the put_* method refuses to write. See registries/DATABASE_DESIGN.md for the full design rationale.

row_id.description: UUID-v4 uniquely identifying this result. Assigned at put time.

kind.description: Which of the four result kinds this row represents. Determines which who_*/where fields are required.

where.description: Genomic location. For pairwise/interval_summary, chrom is required; for candidate_q/candidate_f, candidate_id is required (FK into interval_registry). start_bp/end_bp are optional for chromosome-wide results.

who_1.description: Primary sample group. Required for pairwise, candidate_q, and interval_summary. Null for candidate_f (F matrix has no sample-group dimension).

group_id.description: FK into sample_registry.sample_groups.group_id. Must exist at write time.

group_version.description: Copy of the group's 'created' timestamp at write time. Used to detect stale caches when group membership changes.

who_2.description: Secondary sample group, only for pairwise kind. Otherwise null.

what.description: The statistic or metric this row represents.

stat.description: Named metric. pairwise: fst/dxy/theta_pi/da. candidate_q: Q. candidate_f: F. interval_summary: delta12/entropy/ena/ancestry_q_mean.

K.description: Number of ancestry clusters (for Q/F/ancestry stats). Null for pairwise FST/dxy etc.

file.description: Path to the data file, relative to results_registry root. Must exist at write time.

shape.description: Quick file shape info so audits don't need to re-open the file.

provenance.description: How this result was computed. Required so a reviewer can trace any number back to its source script, config, and input files.

engine.description: Which numerical engine produced the raw result. e.g. instant_q_v13, angsd_v0.940, ngsAdmix_v33.

engine_version.description: Specific build / commit / version string of the engine.

source_script.description: Path (relative to toolkit root) to the R/Python/shell script that made the put_* call.

run_id.description: Identifier for the pipeline run. SLURM job id, ISO timestamp, or anything the submitter sets.

config_hash.description: Short hash of the config file contents at write time. Lets you detect results computed under a different parameter set.

upstream_files.description: List of input file paths that went into this computation (BEAGLE, qopt, fopt, BAM, etc.). Enables DAG reconstruction.

timestamp.description: ISO8601 timestamp of when this row was written.

allOf.description: kind=pairwise requires chrom, who_1 (non-null object), who_2 (non-null object), stat
kind=candidate_q requires candidate_id, who_1, K, stat='Q'
kind=candidate_f requires candidate_id, K, stat='F'. No who (F has no sample-group dimension).
kind=interval_summary requires chrom, start_bp, end_bp, who_1, stat in {delta12, entropy, ena, ancestry_q_mean}
